title: vigna membranacea subsp. membranacea
intro: Vigna membranacea subsp. membranacea, also known as the hairy vine pea or hairy buffalo pea, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Vigna membranacea subsp. membranacea include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
